title: Loops with at most one iteration should be refactored
description: Loops with at most one iteration should be refactoredAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugconfusingbad-practiceWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoAloop statementwith at most one iteration is equivalent to anifstatement; the following block is executed only once.If the initial intention was to conditionally execute the block only once, anifstatement should be used instead. If that was not the initial intention, the block of the loop should be fixed so the block is executed multiple times.A loop statement with at most one iteration can happen when a statement unconditionally transfers control, such as ajump statementor athrow statement, is misplaced inside the loop block.This rule raises when the following statements are misplaced:breakcontinuereturnthrowAvailable I
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.triggering_conditions: Loop with unconditional break/return/throw (at most one iteration)
exclusion_conditions: Loops with conditional break/return inside if statements
logic.reasoning: Detects loops that contain unconditional break, return, or throw statements which cause at most one iteration. Excludes cases where these statements are inside conditionals.

should_trigger: foreach (var item in items) { Console.WriteLine(item); break; }
should_not_trigger: foreach (var item in items) { if (item.IsValid) break; }
